content: State-of-the-art 2024-2025 active learning agent memory systems can be reframed as collective 'curiosity reservoirs' for a population of distributed curiosity-driven agent nodes, where individual agents do not just store their own learning traces but actively curate unlabeled, high-curiosity exploration trajectories to share across the whole agent ecosystem. This structure turns passive memory storage into an active, population-level curiosity allocation mechanism that outperforms isolated intrinsic reward models like ICM or RND.
